refnamediv.refpurpose: Returns an Encoded Polyline from a LineString geometry.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: Returns the geometry as an Encoded Polyline. This format is used by Google Maps with precision=5 and by Open Source Routing Machine with precision=5 and 6.
Optional precision specifies how many decimal places will be preserved in Encoded Polyline. Value should be the same on encoding and decoding, or coordinates will be incorrect.

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.para: Use in conjunction with geography linestring and geography segmentize, and put on google maps
refsection.programlisting: -- the SQL for Boston to San Francisco, segments every 100 KM SELECT ST_AsEncodedPolyline( ST_Segmentize( ST_GeogFromText('LINESTRING(-71.0519 42.4935,-122.4483 37.64)'), 100000)::geometry) As encodedFlightPath;
refsection.para: javascript will look something like this where $ variable you replace with query result
refsection.programlisting:
<script type="text/javascript" src="http://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/js?libraries=geometry"></script>
<script type="text/javascript">
   flightPath = new google.maps.Polyline({
      path:  google.maps.geometry.encoding.decodePath("$encodedFlightPath"),
      map: map,
      strokeColor: '#0000CC',
      strokeOpacity: 1.0,
      strokeWeight: 4
    });
</script>
